title: Leveraged ETF Discipline Ledger
thesis: Leveraged ETFs can express short-term tactical views, but path dependency, volatility drag, and position sizing rules must dominate the decision process.

sources.title: Leveraged and inverse ETF risk bulletin

positions: Maximum planned holding period is five trading days.
Position size must be smaller than the equivalent unlevered exposure.

assumptions.statement: The instrument is used for a defined short-term thesis rather than a long-term allocation.
Volatility conditions are reviewed before entry and daily while open.

catalysts: Index breaks above predefined technical level.
Macro event calendar supports a time-boxed tactical setup.

risks.name: Volatility drag
risks.mitigation: Use only with explicit holding-period limit and daily review.

risks.name: Oversized exposure
risks.mitigation: Cap notional exposure before order entry.

checklist.item: Record entry price, stop, target, and expiry date before trade.
Confirm no earnings, CPI, FOMC, or other event invalidates sizing.
Review realized leverage and tracking after each close.

reviews.summary: Created a discipline ledger for tactical leveraged ETF use.
